that Rasi, the latter takes the shade of the planet. Similarly
a planet conjoined with another will have its natural colour
influenced by that of the other.
Their humours :—The Sun and Mars have Pitta or bile as the
chief humour of their body; the Moon and Venus Vata and Kapha
or wind and phlegm; Saturn Vata or wind; Mercury a mixture of
all the three humours; and Jupiter Kapha or phlegm.
Their seasons :—-You know that there' are six Rtus viz.
Vasanta, (Spring), Grisma (Summer). Varsa (Rainy). Sarad
(Autumn), Hemanta (Dewy), and Sisira (Winter). These belong
to (1) Venus, (2) the Sun and Mars, (3) the Moon. (4) Mercury,
(5) Jupiter and (6) Saturn respectively. Mind you there are
two lords for summer.
Their gems, metals, tastes etc. :—The Sun's precious stone is
Manikya or ruby, the Moon's pearl, Mars' coral, Mercury's
